granted.blastRadius: Updated basics-worker ECS task def env block (additive); revision :14
Created SES config set basics-runtime-outbound, SNS topic basics-ses-events, event destination, worker SES IAM policy; existing SES identity untouched
Additive: new public.workspace_output_quotas table with RLS, new SECURITY DEFINER function increment_output_quota

history.summary: Wired SENDBLUE_API_KEY, SENDBLUE_API_SECRET, SES_FROM_EMAIL, ARTIFACTS_S3_BUCKET into basics-worker task def; deploy clean; task-def revision :14 contains all 4 names.
First deploy attempt failed: Pulumi defaulted deliveryOptions.maxDeliverySeconds to 0; SES requires >= 300.

Option A: ref existing SES identity via data source; created NEW config set basics-runtime-outbound, SNS topic basics-ses-events + topic policy, SNS event destination for BOUNCE/COMPLAINT/DELIVERY, worker SES RolePolicy scoped to identity ARN + config set ARN.

Applied migration automations_a3_output_quotas: workspace_output_quotas table (RLS-enabled) + SECURITY DEFINER function increment_output_quota. Fixture cap=2 calls returned [true,true,false]. Mirrored to api/drizzle.
Added shared/src/activity.ts with output_dispatched/output_failed Zod schemas + discriminated union. Reviewer: ship.
Implemented attach_artifact tool. 10 unit tests + live S3 round-trip (HTTP 200, body match). Reviewer: ship.

Implemented send_email tool via @aws-sdk/client-sesv2 + new QuotaStore abstraction. Cycle 1 reviewer flagged 2 header-injection blockers (CRLF in subject/filename); fixed by Zod refines + base64 body encoding + derived-filename sanitization. Cycle 2 reviewer: ship. 9/9 unit tests pass. Live e2e: SES accepted MessageId 0100019e20065b37-... via basics-runtime-outbound config set.

operatorDecisions.note: Picked safest path that avoids touching production SES MailFromDomain or renaming existing config set.

operatorDecisions.decision: No git push between loop iterations
operatorDecisions.note: Push triggers redundant 45-min CI redeploy. Local commits only; push at phase/plan completion.
